name: Henry Mintzberg
books: ['The Rise and Fall of Strategic Planning', 'Managers Not MBAs', 'Strategy Safari']
notes: Canadian but deeply influential in European management thinking

name: Hermann Simon
books: ['Hidden Champions', 'Confessions of the Pricing Man', 'True Profit']
notes: Founded Simon-Kucher (now $500M+ revenue); most cited German management author

name: Yves Pigneur
books: ['Business Model Generation', 'Value Proposition Design', 'The Invincible Company']
notes: Swiss academic; BMC is the most widely used business framework globally

name: Alex Osterwalder
books: ['Business Model Generation', 'Value Proposition Design', 'Testing Business Ideas']
notes: Swiss; built a product company (Strategyzer) from academic framework

name: Erin Meyer
books: ['The Culture Map', 'No Rules Rules (with Reed Hastings)']
notes: American at INSEAD; The Culture Map became a standard reference

name: Gary Hamel
books: ['Competing for the Future', 'The Future of Management', 'Humanocracy']
notes: American at LBS; one of the most published HBR authors ever

name: Lynda Gratton
books: ['The Shift', 'The 100-Year Life', 'Redesigning Work']
notes: British; World Economic Forum advisor

name: Herminia Ibarra
books: ['Act Like a Leader Think Like a Leader', 'Working Identity']
notes: Cuban-American at LBS; bridges US-European thinking

name: Costas Markides
books: ['Game-Changing Strategies', 'Fast Second', 'All the Right Moves']
notes: Cypriot-British; influential in European strategy teaching

name: Fredmund Malik
books: ['Managing Performing Living', 'Strategy for Managing Complex Systems']
notes: Austrian-Swiss; huge influence in German-speaking world; minimal in Anglo world

name: Kjell Nordström
books: ['Funky Business', 'Karaoke Capitalism']
notes: Swedish provocateur; popular speaker but limited academic output

name: Jonas Ridderstråle
books: ['Funky Business', 'Karaoke Capitalism', 'Fast/Forward']
notes: Swedish; speaking circuit; lighter academic footprint

name: Sumantra Ghoshal
books: ['Managing Across Borders', 'The Individualized Corporation', 'A Bias for Action']
notes: Indian-British at LBS; died 2004; hugely influential

name: Charles Handy
books: ['The Age of Unreason', 'The Empty Raincoat', 'The Second Curve']
notes: Irish-British philosopher of management; BBC radio presenter

name: Manfred Kets de Vries
openalex_name: Manfred F. R. Kets de Vries
books: ['The Leadership Mystique', 'Leaders on the Couch', 'Riding the Leadership Rollercoaster']
notes: Dutch at INSEAD; founder of INSEAD leadership centre

name: W. Chan Kim
books: ['Blue Ocean Strategy', 'Blue Ocean Shift']
notes: Korean at INSEAD; Blue Ocean is one of the top-selling strategy books ever

name: Renée Mauborgne
books: ['Blue Ocean Strategy', 'Blue Ocean Shift']
notes: American at INSEAD; co-creator of Blue Ocean

name: Julian Birkinshaw
books: ['Reinventing Management', 'Fast/Forward', 'Becoming a Better Boss']
notes: British-Canadian; prolific at LBS

name: Nicolai Foss
books: ['Strategy, Economic Organization and the Knowledge Economy']
notes: Danish; strong academic but limited practitioner crossover

name: Freek Vermeulen
books: ['Breaking Bad Habits', 'Business Exposed']
notes: Dutch at LBS; contrarian/provocative style

name: Phanish Puranam
books: ['Corporate Strategy', 'The Microstructure of Organizations']
notes: Indian at INSEAD; more academic than practitioner

name: Gianpiero Petriglieri
notes: Italian at INSEAD; popular on LinkedIn/social media

name: Amy Edmondson
books: ['The Fearless Organization', 'Teaming', 'The Right Kind of Wrong']
notes: American but deeply connected to European executive education

name: Ethan Mollick
books: ['Co-Intelligence']
notes: American but widely read in European tech/business circles
